Washington State · My Health My Data Act

Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy

Last Updated: (set on publish) · Effective Date: (set on publish)

This is a standalone Consumer Health Data Privacy Policy required under Washington's My Health My Data Act (Chapter 19.373 RCW). It supplements our general Privacy Policy. Questions? support@thetrackerapp.io.

1. About this policy

Washington's My Health My Data Act (MHMDA) requires businesses that collect "consumer health data" from Washington residents or in Washington to provide a separate, standalone privacy policy specifically covering that health data. This policy explains what consumer health data TheTrackerApp collects, why, from where, who we share it with, and the rights Washington consumers have.

This policy applies to Washington residents and to individuals whose consumer health data is collected in Washington. Our general Privacy Policy continues to apply to all users for all other personal information.

2. Categories of consumer health data we collect

We collect the following categories of consumer health data. None are required — you control what you log.

Data you log directly

  • Workout & exercise data — exercise type, sets, reps, weight, duration, distance, pace, heart rate (when connected to wearables). MHMDA basis: bodily functions, vital signs, measurements.
  • Nutrition & food logs — food descriptions, calorie intake, macro breakdowns (protein, carbs, fats), meal timing. MHMDA basis: information derived or extrapolated from nonhealth data that identifies health status.
  • Body measurements — weight, body fat percentage, BMI, chest/waist/hip/arm/thigh measurements. MHMDA basis: bodily functions, vital signs, measurements.
  • Hydration data — water and beverage intake. MHMDA basis: bodily functions, measurements.
  • Supplement logs — supplement type, dosage, timing (creatine, protein, vitamins, etc.). MHMDA basis: use of supplements affecting health.
  • Blood work & lab values — cholesterol, A1C, vitamin levels, hormone panels (only if you choose to log them). MHMDA basis: diagnostic testing, treatment.
  • Fitness & body goals — weight loss/gain targets, maintenance calories, body recomposition goals. MHMDA basis: health interventions, health status.
  • Profile health data — age, sex, height, weight, activity level, dietary preferences. MHMDA basis: measurements; derived health indicators (BMR, TDEE, BMI).

Data we infer or derive

  • Estimated maintenance calories (BMR / TDEE) — computed from age, sex, height, weight, activity level
  • Calorie & macro targets — derived from your body goals
  • Nutrition patterns — aggregated macro averages, meal timing patterns
  • Workout patterns — training volume trends, exercise frequency, progressive overload tracking
  • Weight/body trends — weight change rate, body fat trends, BMI trajectory

What we do NOT collect

We do not collect reproductive or sexual health information, gender-affirming care information, precise location tied to health facilities, genetic data, biometric data (face, fingerprint, voiceprint, gait), or mental health / mood data.

3. Why we collect consumer health data

We collect and process consumer health data only to:

  1. Provide the Service — parse your messages, log entries, compute daily/weekly totals, track streaks, generate charts and reports, deliver replies and reminders.
  2. Compute health-derived estimates — BMR, TDEE, macro splits, calorie targets, body measurement trends, BMI.
  3. Operate AI parsing — send message text to Google Gemini (our AI processor) to extract structured entries from natural language. This requires separate consent for Washington residents (see Section 7).
  4. Improve the Service — review aggregated, de-identified metrics about parser accuracy and response latency.
  5. Secure the Service — detect spam, abuse, and platform errors.

We do not collect consumer health data for targeted advertising, sale to data brokers, cross-context behavioral advertising, training AI models (Google's paid API tier does not use inputs for training), or automated decision-making with legal effects.

4. Sources of consumer health data

  • You (directly) — everything you text, type, or send through our messaging channels and dashboard.
  • Your images (Premium tier) — photos of meals, nutrition labels, or scale readings for AI-based parsing.
  • Connected wearables (when authorized) — step count, heart rate, sleep data, workout summaries from Pebble, Fitbit, Whoop, Oura, Apple Watch, Garmin.
  • Our AI processor (Google Gemini) — structured entries extracted from your natural-language messages.
  • Computed on our servers — BMR, TDEE, BMI, calorie targets, macro splits, trends.

5. Categories of consumer health data we share

We share consumer health data only with the processors listed below. We do not sell consumer health data under the MHMDA definition.

Processor What we share Purpose
Google GeminiMessage text (food, workout, body data you text us)AI parsing of natural-language entries
Apple iMessageMessage body (outbound delivery)Delivering replies to iMessage users
TwilioMessage body (outbound SMS/WhatsApp)Delivering replies to SMS/WhatsApp users
TelegramMessage body (outbound delivery)Delivering replies to Telegram users
Google CloudEncrypted backup (ciphertext only)Off-site encrypted backup
CloudflareTLS-encrypted transit dataDDoS protection, CDN

We do not share consumer health data with advertising networks, data brokers, analytics providers, social media platforms, AI training pipelines, or affiliates.

6. Your rights under the MHMDA

If you are a Washington resident (or your consumer health data is collected in Washington), you have these rights:

  • Right to confirm — confirm whether we are collecting, sharing, or selling your consumer health data.
  • Right to access — get a copy of your consumer health data and a list of third parties we've shared it with.
  • Right to delete — delete your consumer health data by deleting your account (permanent removal from live DB; encrypted backups expire within 365 days).
  • Right to withdraw consent — withdraw consent for collection (account deletion) or AI processing (reply /stop ai or toggle in dashboard).
  • Right to appeal — if we decline a request, appeal to support@thetrackerapp.io with "MHMD Appeal" in the subject. Response within 45 days.

To exercise any right, email support@thetrackerapp.io from your account email or verified phone. We authenticate requests, respond within 45 days, and provide information free up to twice per year.

7. Consent under the MHMDA

The MHMDA requires separate, specific, opt-in consent — it cannot be bundled into general terms.

Collection consent: When you sign up, you affirmatively opt in to health data collection through a dedicated consent checkbox.

Sharing consent (AI processing): Washington residents must separately opt in to sharing message content with Google Gemini for AI parsing. If you decline AI, slash commands (/water 20, /log Bench 3x10 at 185) still work without AI. A separate checkbox is shown at signup for WA residents.

8. Geofence prohibition

The MHMDA prohibits geofencing (virtual boundary of ≤2,000 feet) around health care facilities. We do not use geofencing for any purpose. We do not track precise location, send location-based notifications, or collect GPS coordinates.

9. Data security

We protect consumer health data with TLS encryption in transit, full-disk encryption (FileVault) at rest, AES-256-GCM envelope encryption for off-site backups, owner-only database file permissions, and admin access behind server-side tokens. See our Privacy Policy Section 6 for details.

10. Changes to this policy

We will update the "Last Updated" date for changes. Material changes are notified on your primary channel. New sharing categories require fresh consent.

11. Contact

support@thetrackerapp.io
Washington AG: atg.wa.gov
